Autonomous Systems · ROS · Cloud Infrastructure

ADAS Simulation Pipeline

A driver-assistance software stack built on ROS — Adaptive Cruise Control, Automatic Emergency Braking, and Lane Keeping Assist — designed to integrate with the CARLA autonomous driving simulator on cloud GPU infrastructure.

ROS Nodes
CARLA Simulator
Docker
Cloud GPU (Paperspace/RunPod)

Capabilities
Key Features
▶Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)

Maintains a target speed while backing off the throttle to preserve a safe following distance — implemented as a proportional speed/distance controller using fused radar data.

▶Automatic Emergency Braking (AEB)

Computes time-to-collision in real-time from radar distance and relative velocity; triggers full braking the instant TTC drops below a configurable 1.5-second safety threshold.

▶Lane Keeping Assist (LKA)

OpenCV-based lane detection feeds a proportional steering controller that continuously corrects for lane offset and heading error through curves.

▶Sensor Fusion Node

Combines radar distance/velocity readings with camera-based lane detection into a single fused message consumed by all downstream control nodes.

▶Vehicle Control Arbitration

Combines ACC, AEB, and LKA outputs into a single vehicle command — AEB is given hard override priority over ACC and LKA whenever a collision risk is detected, matching real-world ADAS safety priority design.

▶Multi-Cloud GPU Deployment

Containerized and deployed the CARLA simulator across multiple cloud GPU providers (Paperspace, RunPod), debugging real cross-platform issues — ARM64 vs x86_64, Python version mismatches, GPU rendering compatibility.

System Design
Architecture
CARLA Simulator (cloud GPU)
↓
ROS Bridge — camera, radar, vehicle control
↓
Sensor Fusion Node
↓
ACC Node  |  LKA Node  |  AEB Node
↓
Vehicle Control Arbitration (AEB overrides ACC/LKA)
↓
CARLA vehicle
How It Works
Technical Implementation
[01]
ROS Node ArchitectureEach ADAS function — ACC, AEB, LKA — runs as an independent, message-passing ROS node, mirroring how production ADAS software stacks separate concerns for testability and fault isolation.
[02]
Control LogicPID-style speed/distance control for ACC, time-to-collision math for AEB, and proportional steering correction for LKA — all implemented and unit-verified against simulated sensor data before live integration.
[03]
ContainerizationFull multi-node ROS stack containerized with Docker for reproducible deployment across local and cloud environments.
[04]
Cloud GPU InfrastructureDeployed and debugged the CARLA simulator across Paperspace and RunPod GPU instances — diagnosing real infrastructure issues rather than developing against a single fixed environment.
[05]
Interactive Demo LayerBuilt a standalone browser-based dashboard running the identical control math as the ROS nodes, to demonstrate system behavior independent of CARLA's GPU-specific rendering pipeline.
Build Status
✅All 5 ADAS modules built & verified against simulated sensor data
✅CARLA simulator deployed & running on cloud GPU
🟡Live CARLA sensor stream integration — blocked on GPU-tier-specific rendering issue, documented with resume steps
